title: Tetragonolobus conjugatus
taxonomy: Tetragonolobus conjugatus is classified as a species of flowering plant. It belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. The plant is further classified as a member of the genus Tetragonolobus, which is a small group of legumes native to Australia. Tetragonolobus conjugatus is considered to be a species of pea or bean. This classification helps to understand the plant's relationships and characteristics within the broader plant kingdom.
uses: Tetragonolobus conjugatus, also known as horse bean, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its seeds are rich in protein, fiber, and other nutrients, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. Traditionally, Tetragonolobus conjugatus has been used to help lower cholesterol levels and improve digestion. The plant's seeds have also been used to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Tetragonolobus conjugatus for medicinal purposes.

intro: Tetragonolobus conjugatus, also known as horse gram, is a perennial legume native to the Indian subcontinent and Africa, valued for its nutritional and medicinal properties. The plant is rich in health benefits, including reducing cholesterol levels, aiding in weight loss, and exhibiting potential anti-inflammatory and anti-cancer properties. The bioactive constituents of horse gram include proteins, fiber, and various polyphenolic compounds, including tetragonolol and conjugatin, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, horse gram is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powder, often used in Ayurvedic medicine to support digestive health and manage various health conditions.
